sections.heading: Overview
sections.content: Scanning is what happens when you run `cortex init` or `cortex build`. It reads your Python source files and DocJSON docs, extracts structured metadata, and writes it to the index database. `cortex init` does a full scan with baseline reset; `cortex build` is discovery-only (adds new nodes, preserves existing staleness signals).

sections.heading: Quick Start
sections.content:
```bash
# First-time setup (creates DB, full scan, establishes baselines)
cortex init .

# After adding new files (discovery-only — won't overwrite existing nodes)
cortex build .

# Reset to clean baseline (re-runnable — prompts for confirmation)
cortex init .
```

sections.heading: What Gets Scanned
sections.content:
- **Python files (*.py):** Every function, async function, and class method becomes an `atomic_process` node. Every module becomes a `composite_process` node. dFlow decorators are detected and stored.
- **DocJSON files (docs/**/*.json):** Each file becomes a `document` node. Each section within becomes a child document node. Links in sections generate `documents` edges.
- **Markdown files:** Indexed as document nodes with whole-file hashing.
- **External imports:** Third-party packages detected in import statements become `entity` nodes with `subtype=external_package`.

sections.heading: Configuration & Options
sections.content:
| Option | Description | Default |
|---|---|---|
| `--id <prefix>` | Custom project ID used in node IDs (both commands) | Directory name |

`cortex build` has no `--full` flag. To reset baselines, use `cortex init`.

The scanner respects `.gitignore` patterns and skips common non-source directories (`__pycache__`, `.git`, `node_modules`, etc.).

sections.heading: Best Practices & Troubleshooting
sections.content:
### Best Practices
- Run `cortex init .` on a new project for first-time setup
- Run `cortex build .` after pulling new code to discover new nodes
- Use `cortex init .` to reset baselines after resolving all stale nodes

### Common Issues
- **Problem:** A function shows as STRUCTURAL_DRIFT after renaming
- **Solution:** The old node is orphaned. `cortex build .` creates the new node; manually reconnect edges with `cortex link`.

- **Problem:** Warnings about files with syntax errors
- **Solution:** The scanner skips unparseable files. Fix the syntax error and re-run `cortex build`.
